curriculum.subject: Science
curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 26 chapters, and 33 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Describe the particulate nature of matter.
Explain characteristics of solid, liquid, and gas states.
Analyze change of state processes including melting, freezing, evaporation, condensation, and sublimation.
Distinguish between elements, compounds, and mixtures.
Differentiate heterogeneous and homogeneous mixtures, colloids, and suspensions based on properties like transparency and stability.
Apply the Law of Chemical Combination to represent chemical interactions.
Describe atomic structure including sub-atomic particles (electrons, protons, neutrons) and Bohr's model.
Calculate atomic number, mass number, valency, isotopes, and isobars.
Explain the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, chloroplasts, etc.) as structural basis for living organisms.
Differentiate between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.
Analyze structure and functions of animal tissues (muscle fibers) and plant tissues (meristematic and permanent).

units.description: Matter in Our Surroundings, Is Matter Around Us Pure, Atoms and Molecules
topics: States of matter, change of state
Sub-atomic particles, Models of atom, Isotopes and Isobars

units.description: Cell - Basic Unit of life, Tissues, Organs, Organ System, Organism
Motion, Force and Newton's laws, Gravitation, Floatation, Work, Energy and Power, Sound

raw_nodes.title: SCIENCE Subject Code – 086 Classes IX and X (2025-26)
raw_nodes.source_text: 1 SCIENCE Subject Code – 086 Classes IX and X (2025-26) Science Education aims to achieve Scientific understanding of the natural and physical world; Capacities for scientific inquiry; Understanding the evolution of scientific knowledge; Interdisciplinary understanding between science and other curricular areas; Understanding of the relationship between Science, Technology and, Society; Scientific temper and Creativity. The present syllabus has been designed around seven broad themes viz. Food; Materials; The World of the Living; How Things Work; Moving Things, People and Ideas; Natural Phenomenon and Natural Resources.

raw_nodes.title: Curricular Goals
raw_nodes.source_text: The Curricular Goals of Science at the Secondary Stage move from the concrete nature of the Middle Stage towards abstraction - from perceptual and practical concepts to theoretical concepts. The Learning Standards (Curricular Goals and Competencies) for Science as an integrated curricular area, in alignment with the National Curriculum Framework 2023 are as follows: CG - 1 Explores the world of matter... C - 7.2 States questions related to matters in the curriculum...

raw_nodes.title: General Instructions for Assessment
raw_nodes.source_text: It is important to note that the Curricular Goals are interdependent... General Instructions for Assessment: 1. There will be an Annual Examination based on the entire syllabus. 2. The Annual Examination will be of 80 marks and 20 marks weightage shall be for Internal Assessment.

raw_nodes.title: Internal Assessment Details
raw_nodes.source_text: For Internal Assessment: i) There will be Periodic Assessment that would include... ii) For 5 marks - Practical / Laboratory work... iii) For 5 marks - Portfolio...

raw_nodes.title: COURSE STRUCTURE CLASS IX (2025-26)
raw_nodes.source_text: Time: 03 Hours Marks: 80 Unit No. Unit Marks I Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour 25 II Organization in the Living World 22 III Motion, Force and Work 27 IV Food; Food Production 06 Total 80 Internal assessment 20 Grand Total 100

raw_nodes.source_text: Matter in Our Surroundings... Is Matter Around Us Pure... Atoms and Molecules...

raw_nodes.source_text: Motion... Force and Newton's laws... Gravitation... Floatation... Work, Energy and Power... Sound:

raw_nodes.source_text: The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination.

raw_nodes.title: COURSE STRUCTURE CLASS X (2025-26)
raw_nodes.source_text: Time: 03 Hours Marks: 80 Unit No. Unit Marks I Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour 25 II World of Living 25 III Natural Phenomena 12 IV Effects of Current 13 V Natural Resources 05 Total 80 Internal assessment 20 Grand Total 100

raw_nodes.source_text: Chemical Reactions and Equations... Acids, Bases and Salts... Metals and Non-metals... Carbon and its Compounds...

raw_nodes.source_text: Competencies Total Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding 50 % Application of Knowledge/Concepts 30 % Formulate, Analyze, Evaluate and Create 20 %

raw_nodes.title: Internal Assessment (Class X)
raw_nodes.source_text: Periodic Assessment - 05 marks + 05 marks Subject Enrichment (Practical Work) - 05 marks Portfolio - 05 marks

headings: Definition of matter; Particulate Nature of Matter; States of Matter: solid, liquid and gas and their characteristics; change of state- melting (absorption of heat), freezing, evaporation (cooling by evaporation), condensation, sublimation.

Elements, compounds and mixtures. Heterogeneous and homogenous mixtures, colloids and suspensions. Physical and chemical changes (excluding separating the components of a mixture); Pure and Impure substances.

Atoms and molecules, Law of Chemical Combination, Chemical formula of common compounds, Atomic and molecular masses. Structure of atom: Sub-atomic particles: Electrons, protons and neutrons, Models of atom; Valency, Atomic Number and Mass Number, Isotopes and Isobars.
subtopics: States of matter, change of state
Sub-atomic particles, Models of atom, Isotopes and Isobars

headings: Cell as a basic unit of life; prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, multicellular organisms; cell membrane and cell wall, cell organelles and cell inclusions; chloroplast, mitochondria, vacuoles, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus; nucleus, chromosomes - basic structure, number.
Structure and functions of animal and plant tissues (only four types of tissues in animals; Meristematic and Permanent tissues in plants).
key_concepts: Cell as basic unit of life

headings: Distance and displacement, velocity; uniform and non-uniform motion along a straight line; acceleration, distance-time and velocity-time graphs for uniform motion and uniformly accelerated motion, elementary idea of uniform circular motion.
Force and Motion, Newton's Laws of Motion, Action and Reaction forces, Inertia of a body, Inertia and mass, Momentum, Force and Acceleration.
Gravitation; Universal Law of Gravitation, Force of Gravitation of the earth (gravity), Acceleration due to Gravity; Mass and Weight; Free fall.
Thrust and Pressure. Archimedes' Principle; Buoyancy.
Work done by a Force, Energy, power; Kinetic and Potential energy; Lawof conservation of energy (excluding commercial unit of Energy).
Nature of sound and its propagation in various media, speed of sound, range of hearing in humans; ultrasound; reflection of sound; echo.
Plant and animal breeding and selection for quality improvement and management; Use of fertilizersand manures; Protection from pests and diseases; Organic farming.

Chemical reactions, Chemical equation, Balanced chemical equation, types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation, endothermic exothermic reactions, oxidation and reduction.

Acids and Bases – definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH– ions, identification using indicators, chemical properties, examples and uses, neutralization, concept of pH scale... importance of pH in everyday life; preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris.
Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds; Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention.

Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon, Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature... Chemical properties of carbon compounds (combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reaction). Ethanol and Ethanoic acid...

explicit_chapters: Control and co-ordination in animals and plants
headings: 'Living Being'. Basic concept of nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals.
Tropic movements in plants; Introduction of plant hormones; Control and co-ordination in animals: Nervous system; Voluntary, involuntary and reflex action; Chemical co-ordination: animal hormones.
Reproduction in animals and plants (asexual and sexual) reproductive health - need and methods of family planning. Safe sex vs HIV/AIDS. Child bearing and women's health.
Heredity; Mendel's contribution- Laws for inheritance of traits: Sex determination; brief introduction.

explicit_chapters: Reflection of light by curved surfaces
headings: Images formed by spherical mirrors, centre of curvature, principal axis, principal focus, focal length, mirror formula (Derivation not required), magnification.

Laws of refraction, refractive index. Refraction of light by spherical lens; Image formed by spherical lenses; Lens formula (Derivation not required); Magnification. Power of a lens. Functioning of a lens in human eye, defects of vision and their corrections...
Refraction of light through a prism, dispersion of light, scattering of light...
Electric current, potential difference and electric current. Ohm's law; Resistance, Resistivity, Factors on which the resistance of a conductor depends.
Series combination of resistors, parallel combination of resistors... Heating effect of electric current... Electric power...
Magnetic field, field lines, field due to a current carrying conductor, field due to current carrying coil or solenoid; Force on current carrying conductor, Fleming's Left Hand Rule...
Eco-system, Environmental problems, Ozone depletion, waste production and their solutions. Biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances.

formative_only_content.title: Health and Diseases (Class IX)
formative_only_content.source_text: The following topics are included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments. The reduces academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning... Health and its failure. Infectious and Non-infectious diseases, their causes and manifestation. Diseases caused by microbes (Virus, Bacteria and Protozoans) and their prevention; Principles of treatment and prevention. Pulse Polio programmes.
formative_only_content.related_chapter: Cell - Basic Unit of life / Tissues

formative_only_content.title: Elementary idea of conservation of Momentum (Class IX)
formative_only_content.source_text: The following topic is included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments. The reduces academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning... Elementary idea of conservation of Momentum

practicals.title: Preparation of solutions (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 1. Preparation of: Unit-I a) a true solution of common salt, sugar and alum b) a suspension of soil, chalk powder and fine sand in water c) a colloidal solution of starch in water and egg albumin/milk in water
practicals.related_unit: Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)

practicals.title: Preparation of mixture/compound (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 2. Preparation of Unit-I a) A mixture b) A compound using iron filings and sulphur powder
practicals.related_unit: Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)

practicals.title: Physical/Chemical changes reactions (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 3. Perform the following reactions... Iron with copper sulphate solution... Burning of magnesium ribbon...
practicals.related_unit: Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)

practicals.title: Stained temporary mounts (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 4. Preparation of stained temporary mounts of (a) onion peel, (b) human cheek cells
practicals.related_unit: Organization in the Living World (Unit II)

practicals.title: Identification of tissues (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 5. Identification of Parenchyma, Collenchyma and Sclerenyma tissues in plants... muscle fibers...
practicals.related_unit: Organization in the Living World (Unit II)

practicals.title: Melting/Boiling point determination (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 6. Determination of the melting point of ice and the boiling point of water.
practicals.related_unit: Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)

practicals.title: Laws of reflection of sound (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 7. Verification of the laws of reflection of sound.

practicals.title: Density determination (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 8. Determination of the density of solid... by using a spring balance...

practicals.title: Archimedes' Principle verification (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 9. Establishing the relation between the loss in weight of a solid when fully immersed...

practicals.title: Speed of pulse propagation (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 10. Determination of the speed of a pulse propagated through a stretched string/slinky

practicals.title: Conservation of mass verification (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 11. Verification of the law of conservation of mass in a chemical reaction.
practicals.related_unit: Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)

practicals.title: pH determination (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 1. A. Finding the pH of the following samples by using pH paper/universal indicator: Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution...

practicals.title: Properties of acids/bases reactions (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 2. Performing and observing the following reactions... Action of water on quicklime...

practicals.title: Reactivity series experiment (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 3. Observing the action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al metals on salt solutions...

practicals.title: Ohm's Law verification (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 4. Studying the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I)... plotting a graph between V and I.

practicals.title: Equivalent resistance (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 5. Determination of the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel.

practicals.title: Stomata observation (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 6. Preparing a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.

practicals.title: CO2 in respiration experiment (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 7. Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.

practicals.title: Acetic acid properties (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 8. Study of the following properties of acetic acid... Odour, solubility in water...

practicals.title: Soap cleaning capacity (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 9. Study of the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water.

practicals.title: Focal length determination (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 10. Determination of the focal length of: Concave mirror, Convex lens...

practicals.title: Glass slab refraction tracing (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 11. Tracing the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab...

practicals.title: Binary fission/Budding study (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 12. Studying (a) binary fission in Amoeba, and (b) budding in yeast...

practicals.title: Prism refraction tracing (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 13. Tracing the path of the rays of light through a glass prism.

practicals.title: Embryo identification (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 14. Identification of the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed...

activities.title: Periodic Assessment Methods
activities.source_text: Diverse methods of assessment as per the need of the class dynamics and curriculum transaction. These may include - short tests, oral test, quiz, concept maps, projects, posters, presentations and enquiry based scientific investigations etc.

projects.title: Portfolio
projects.source_text: For 5 marks - Portfolio that includes classwork and other sample of student's work.

marks_distribution: Annual Examination: 80 Marks (Class IX and X)
excluded_or_non_assessed_content: Information in NCERT text book boxes (not assessed in year-end examination)
Separating the components of a mixture (Physical and chemical changes section - Class IX Unit I)
Commercial unit of Energy (Work, Energy and Power section - Class IX Unit III)
Definition relating to logarithm for pH scale
teacher_notes: The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination.
Formative topics (Health and Diseases; Conservation of Momentum) are included to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.
Schools can integrate formative topics with existing chapters as they align well.

headings: Definition of matter; Particulate Nature of Matter; States of Matter: solid, liquid and gas and their characteristics; change of state- melting (absorption of heat), freezing, evaporation (cooling by evaporation), condensation, sublimation.

Elements, compounds and mixtures. Heterogeneous and homogenous mixtures, colloids and suspensions. Physical and chemical changes (excluding separating the components of a mixture); Pure and Impure substances.

Atoms and molecules, Law of Chemical Combination, Chemical formula of common compounds, Atomic and molecular masses. Structure of atom: Sub-atomic particles: Electrons, protons and neutrons, Models of atom; Valency, Atomic Number and Mass Number, Isotopes and Isobars.
subtopics: States of matter, change of state
Sub-atomic particles, Models of atom, Isotopes and Isobars

topics: States of matter, change of state
Sub-atomic particles, Models of atom, Isotopes and Isobars

headings: Cell as a basic unit of life; prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, multicellular organisms; cell membrane and cell wall, cell organelles and cell inclusions; chloroplast, mitochondria, vacuoles, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus; nucleus, chromosomes - basic structure, number.
Structure and functions of animal and plant tissues (only four types of tissues in animals; Meristematic and Permanent tissues in plants).
key_concepts: Cell as basic unit of life

headings: Distance and displacement, velocity; uniform and non-uniform motion along a straight line; acceleration, distance-time and velocity-time graphs for uniform motion and uniformly accelerated motion, elementary idea of uniform circular motion.
Force and Motion, Newton's Laws of Motion, Action and Reaction forces, Inertia of a body, Inertia and mass, Momentum, Force and Acceleration.
Gravitation; Universal Law of Gravitation, Force of Gravitation of the earth (gravity), Acceleration due to Gravity; Mass and Weight; Free fall.
Thrust and Pressure. Archimedes' Principle; Buoyancy.
Work done by a Force, Energy, power; Kinetic and Potential energy; Lawof conservation of energy (excluding commercial unit of Energy).
Nature of sound and its propagation in various media, speed of sound, range of hearing in humans; ultrasound; reflection of sound; echo.
Plant and animal breeding and selection for quality improvement and management; Use of fertilizersand manures; Protection from pests and diseases; Organic farming.

formative_content_refs.title: Cell as basic unit of life

headings: Chemical reactions, Chemical equation, Balanced chemical equation, types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation, endothermic exothermic reactions, oxidation and reduction.

Acids and Bases – definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH– ions, identification using indicators, chemical properties, examples and uses, neutralization, concept of pH scale... importance of pH in everyday life; preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris.
Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds; Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention.

Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon, Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature... Chemical properties of carbon compounds (combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reaction). Ethanol and Ethanoic acid...

explicit_chapters: Control and co-ordination in animals and plants
headings: 'Living Being'. Basic concept of nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals.
Tropic movements in plants; Introduction of plant hormones; Control and co-ordination in animals: Nervous system; Voluntary, involuntary and reflex action; Chemical co-ordination: animal hormones.
Reproduction in animals and plants (asexual and sexual) reproductive health - need and methods of family planning. Safe sex vs HIV/AIDS. Child bearing and women's health.
Heredity; Mendel's contribution- Laws for inheritance of traits: Sex determination; brief introduction.

activities.title: Periodic Assessment Methods
activities.source_text: Diverse methods of assessment as per the need of the class dynamics and curriculum transaction. These may include - short tests, oral test, quiz, concept maps, projects, posters, presentations and enquiry based scientific investigations etc.

projects.source_text: For 5 marks - Portfolio that includes classwork and other sample of student's work.

marks_distribution: Annual Examination: 80 Marks (Class IX and X)
excluded_or_non_assessed_content: Information in NCERT text book boxes (not assessed in year-end examination)
Separating the components of a mixture (Physical and chemical changes section - Class IX Unit I)
Commercial unit of Energy (Work, Energy and Power section - Class IX Unit III)
Definition relating to logarithm for pH scale
teacher_notes: The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination.
Formative topics (Health and Diseases; Conservation of Momentum) are included to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.
Schools can integrate formative topics with existing chapters as they align well.

competency_groups.title: Explores the world of matter, its interactions, and properties at the atomic level
competency_groups.description: C - 1.1 Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table, and explains how compounds (including carbon compounds) are formed based on atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency). C - 1.2 Investigates the nature and properties of chemical substances (distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation, types and properties of mixtures, solutions, colloids, and suspensions). C - 1.3 Describes and represents chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations (acid and base, metal, and non-metal, reversible, and irreversible)
competencies: C-1.1: Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table
C-1.2: Investigates nature and properties of chemical substances including mixtures, solutions, colloids, suspensions
C-1.3: Represents chemical interactions using symbols and equations

competency_groups.title: Explores the physical world around them, and understands scientific principles and laws based on observations and analysis
competency_groups.description: C - 2.1 Applies Newton's laws to explain the effect of forces (change in state of motion – displacement and direction, velocity and acceleration, uniform circular motion, acceleration due to gravity) and analyses graphical and mathematical representations of motion in one dimension. C - 2.2 Explains the relationship between mass and weight using universal law of gravitation and connect it to laws of motion. C - 2.3 Manipulates the position of object and properties of lenses (focus, centre of curvature) to observe image characteristics and correspondence with a ray diagram, and extends this understanding to a combination of lenses (telescope, microscope). C - 2.4 Manipulates and analyses different characteristics of the circuit (current, voltage, resistance) and mathematises their relationship (Ohm's law), and applies it to everyday usage (electricity bill, short circuit, safety measures). C - 2.5 Defines work in scientific terms, and represents the relationship between potential and kinetic energy (conservation of energy) in mathematical expressions. C - 2.6 Demonstrates the principle of mechanical advantage by constructing simple machines (system of levers and pulleys). C - 2.7 Describes the origin and properties of sound (wavelength, frequency, amplitude) and differences in what we hear as it propagates through different instruments
competencies: C-2.1: Applies Newton's laws to explain effects of forces and analyses motion graphs
C-2.2: Explains relationship between mass and weight using universal law of gravitation
C-2.3: Manipulates lenses to observe image characteristics and ray diagrams
C-2.4: Analyses circuit characteristics (current, voltage, resistance) and Ohm's law applications
C-2.5: Defines work and represents relationship between potential and kinetic energy
C-2.6: Demonstrates mechanical advantage using simple machines
C-2.7: Describes origin and properties of sound

competency_groups.title: Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
competency_groups.description: C - 3.1 Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi-permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes. C - 3.2 Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition (photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals), transport (transport of water in plants; circulation in animals), exchange of materials (respiration and excretion), and reproduction. C - 3.3 Describes mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation (as changes in the sequence of DNA)
competencies: C-3.1: Explains role of cellular components and semi-permeability
C-3.2: Analyses similarities/differences in life processes including nutrition, transport, respiration, excretion, reproduction
C-3.3: Describes mechanisms of heredity (DNA, genes) and variation

competency_groups.title: Explores interconnectedness between organisms and their environment
competency_groups.description: C - 4.1 Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic or heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five-kingdoms. C - 4.2 Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms). C - 4.3 Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level. C - 4.4 Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level (using models and/or simulations). C - 4.5 Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection in terms of changes: in allele frequency in population, structure, and function of organisms
competencies: C-4.1: Classifies organisms into five kingdoms based on cellular diversity and ecological role
C-4.2: Illustrates levels of organisation from molecules to organisms
C-4.3: Analyses biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems/biomes
C-4.4: Analyses patterns of inheritance using Mendel's laws
C-4.5: Analyses evidences of evolution and natural selection

competency_groups.title: Draws linkages between scientific knowledge and knowledge across other curricular areas
competency_groups.description: C - 5.1 Explores how literature and the arts have influenced Science. C - 5.2 Examines a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and ethics (e.g., Marie Curie, Jenner, treatment of patients with mental illness, the story of the atomic bomb, green revolution and GMOs, conservation of biodiversity). C - 5.3 Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects (sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music; use of muscles in dance form and sports)
competencies: C-5.1: Explores influence of literature and arts on Science
C-5.2: Examines case studies linking Science with Social Sciences and ethics
C-5.3: Applies scientific principles to phenomena in other subjects

competency_groups.title: Understands and appreciates the contribution of India through history and the present times to the overall field of Science, including the disciplines that constitute it
competency_groups.description: C - 6.1 Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters (concepts, explanations, methods) that are studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner
competencies: C-6.1: Explains significant Indian contributions to Science concepts, explanations, and methods

competency_groups.title: Develops awareness of the most current discoveries, ideas, and frontiers in all areas of scientific knowledge in order to appreciate that Science is ever evolving, and that there are still many unanswered questions
competency_groups.description: C - 7.1 States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied, ranging from mere familiarity to conceptual understanding of the matter as appropriate to the developmental stage of the students. C - 7.2 States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well recognised to be inadequate
competencies: C-7.1: States concepts representing most current scientific understanding
C-7.2: Identifies unanswered questions and areas of inadequate scientific understanding

competency_groups.title: Explores the nature of Science by doing Science
competency_groups.description: C - 8.1 Develops accurate and appropriate models (including geometric, mathematical, graphical) to represent real-life events and phenomena using scientific principles and use these models to manipulate variables and predict results. C - 8.2 Designs and implements a plan for scientific inquiry (formulates hypotheses, makes predictions, identifies variables, accurately uses scientific instruments, represents data, primary and secondary, in multiple modes, draws inferences based on data and understanding of scientific concepts, theories, laws, and principles, communicates findings using scientific terminology)
competencies: C-8.1: Develops models to represent phenomena and predict results
C-8.2: Designs and implements plans for scientific inquiry including hypothesis formulation and data representation

question_paper_design: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (50%) - State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize
Application of Knowledge/Concepts (30%) - Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish
Formulate, Analyze, Evaluate and Create (20%) - Interpret, analyze, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct
bloom_levels: Level 2 (Understanding & Application): Describe, outline, summarize, calculate, illustrate, explain
Level 3 (Higher Order Thinking Skills): Interpret, analyze, compare, contrast, evaluate, discuss, construct
internal_assessment: Periodic Assessment: 5 Marks (Average of best two tests)
Diverse Methods of Assessment: 5 Marks (Short tests, oral test, quiz, concept maps, projects, posters, presentations)
practical_assessment: Continuous assessment throughout the year with maintained records
project_assessment: Portfolio: 5 Marks (Includes classwork and other sample of student's work)

learning_outcomes.topic: States of Matter, Change of State
outcomes: Describe the particulate nature of matter.
Explain characteristics of solid, liquid, and gas states.
Analyze change of state processes including melting, freezing, evaporation, condensation, and sublimation.
Distinguish between elements, compounds, and mixtures.
Differentiate heterogeneous and homogeneous mixtures, colloids, and suspensions based on properties like transparency and stability.
Classify physical and chemical changes.
Apply the Law of Chemical Combination to represent chemical interactions.
Describe atomic structure including sub-atomic particles (electrons, protons, neutrons) and Bohr's model.
Calculate atomic number, mass number, valency, isotopes, and isobars.
Explain the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, chloroplasts, etc.) as structural basis for living organisms.
Differentiate between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.
Analyze structure and functions of animal tissues (muscle fibers) and plant tissues (meristematic and permanent).
Apply Newton's laws to explain effects of forces on motion.
Analyze graphical representations (distance-time, velocity-time) for uniform and non-uniform motion.
Explain inertia, momentum, force, and acceleration.
Analyze action-reaction forces.
Explain the relationship between mass and weight using the universal law of gravitation.
Analyze acceleration due to gravity.
Apply Archimedes' principle to establish relations between loss in weight of a solid immersed in fluids.
Define work scientifically.
Represent the relationship between potential and kinetic energy mathematically.
Describe origin and properties of sound (wavelength, frequency, amplitude).
Represent chemical interactions using balanced equations.
Classify reactions as combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation, endothermic, or exothermic.
Define acids and bases in terms of H+ and OH- ions.
Explain the concept of pH scale and neutralization.
Analyze properties of metals and non-metals.
Explain formation and properties of ionic compounds.
Explain covalent bonding and the versatile nature of carbon.
Classify hydrocarbons (saturated/unsaturated) and homologous series.
Analyze similarities and differences in life processes (nutrition, respiration, transport, excretion) between plants and animals.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Control and co-ordination in animals and plants
outcomes: Explain control mechanisms involving the nervous system (voluntary, involuntary, reflex) and chemical coordination via hormones.
Describe asexual and sexual reproduction in plants and animals.
Analyze patterns of inheritance using Mendel's laws.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Reflection of light by curved surfaces
outcomes: Manipulate object position to observe image characteristics corresponding with ray diagrams.
Apply laws of refraction to spherical lenses.
Explain dispersion, scattering, and applications in daily life.
Mathematize the relationship between potential difference, current, and resistance (Ohm's law).
Describe magnetic fields and field lines due to conductors.
Analyze environmental problems including ozone depletion, waste production, and biodegradable vs non-biodegradable substances.
